subject: Astronomy & Space
title: Sputnik and the Dawn of the Space Age
description: Learn Sputnik and the Dawn of the Space Age in one minute.

hook.text: On October 4, 1957, the Soviet Union launched Sputnik. It was the first artificial satellite—and it terrified the West.

buildup.text: Sputnik was a polished metal sphere just 58 cm across. It orbited Earth every 96 minutes, beeping on radio frequencies.

discovery.text: Anyone with a radio could hear it. The beeping proved that space was reachable—and the Soviets got there first.

twist.text: Sputnik's batteries died after 21 days. It burned up on reentry in January 1958. But it had already changed the world.

climax.text: The US created NASA in 1958 in direct response. The space race—and the modern space age—had begun.

punchline.text: A beeping metal ball. Twenty-one days in orbit. The space age began.

quiz.question: What was the immediate consequence of Sputnik's launch for the United States?
options: The creation of NASA and the start of the Space Race
The first crewed mission to the Moon
The development of the first space telescope
